violations.text: Pho broth, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Food item was observed at 62 degrees Fahrenheit during the time of inspection and was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pho broth was placed in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling methods discussed with the PIC during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by live activity in the prep room. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to work with pest control to establishment plan of action to treat pest issue. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of various dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed from containers during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

A hose is connected to the faucet next to the sink by cookline. This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The presence of rodents and small flies is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings, and live insects observed during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Plumbing in the basement storage area was observed to be leaking into a large refuse receptacle. Improper repair or maintenance of any portion of the plumbing system may result in potential health hazards such as cross connections, backflow, or leakage. Improper repair or maintenance may result in the creation of obnoxious odors or nuisances, and may result in contamination of food, equipment, and single-service or single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2024

Excess accumulation of rodent droppings was observed throughout the dining room area. rodent droppings and other debris and food residue are to be cleaned at a frequency to prevent further accumulation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area and the exterior storage area by the back door. Overgrown foliage was observed surrounding exterior of establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions for pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Food contact surfaces and nonfood of ice machine were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The presence of rodents and small flies is not controlled as evidenced by rodent nesting materials, rodent droppings, and live insects observed during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
Drain plugs are lacking at 3 bay sink. Drain plugs are required to properly clean and sanitize dishes. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
Shelves in walk-in cooler were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
Proper air gap is lacking at food prep sink in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. handsink in warewashing area (drainage pipe not connected) 2. prerinse hose and faucet (leaking) 3. junctures of 3 bay basins (welding observed in poor repair) 4. 3 bay sink faucet (leaking) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
The condensate walk-in cooler condenser is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The following was observed with an accumulation of soil residue and debris: 1. floors, walls ceiling in walk-in cooler 2. kitchen hood 3. holes in walls throughout the warewashing area The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area and the exterior storage area by the back door. Overgrown foliage was observed surrounding exterior of establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions for pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
The floor tile grout in warewashing area is in poor repair and/or not applied. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The coving in the warewashing room is constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent (water damage observed). The materials for indoor coving must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Written procedures for controlling Noro Virus was provided at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The presence of Mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout kitchen and dinning room of establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment shall have a pest control assess the building and property for rodent activity. Entire kitchen and dinning room shall be professionally cleaned and sanitized. Property shall be cleaned all trash, and equipment shall be removed as this created a harborage situation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

Ice Machine condensate pipe was observed draining into a sump pump that is filled with water. Pipe shall not be long enough so that it is submerged in water, this can create a back-siphonage. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
Establishment shall have a receptable with a cover in the restroom New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The floors and walls throughout the kitchen were observed lacking in proper coving. Wooden base boards were observed, or coving was lacking altogether. A non-absorbent, smooth, easily cleanable, durable material shall be installed in the entire kitchen where food is being prepared, behind sinks, dish machine and high moisture areas. Splash guard shall be installed between mop sink and 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
